mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, discount rates on future earnings decline and equity multiples expand, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not precede higher 20-day returns or if cuts occur alongside negative equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the conditional mean return is not above the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates labor-market deterioration and impending earnings downside, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is rising quickly rather than merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer demand and near-term revenue support for large-cap equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal should fail when sales growth is distorted by inflation spikes rather than real demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly-aligned 5-day forward SPX returns are not higher in the top tercile of RSAFS growth than in the bottom tercile, or if the sign reverses after controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation surges.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in INDPRO and PAYEMS indicates rising industrial output with broadening labor input, which should support cyclical earnings and lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if output rises without payroll gains or if payroll gains come with contracting production.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day forward SPX returns, or if positive returns do not occur in at least 60% of months where both series improve sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply, future residential investment and housing-linked consumption weaken, which should drag S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The signal is clearest during housing slowdowns and fails when housing weakness is offset by stronger nonresidential demand.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than half of months with year-over-year HOUST declines above 8%, or if the average return in those periods is not below baseline over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling CPIAUCSL and a still-restrictive FEDFUNDS rate signals disinflation without immediate policy relief, which should compress growth expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect reverses only if the Fed is already cutting aggressively.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term does not improve predictive accuracy versus either proxy alone, or if 5-day forward SPX returns are not negative when CPIAUCSL decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains above 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor-market cooling regime, a simultaneous rise in UNRATE and slowdown in PAYEMS growth signals weakening income and profit expectations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month. The pattern should not be active when unemployment rises but payrolls accelerate.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor signal yields nonnegative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the combined signal is no better than a single-series UNRATE rule over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDPC1 relative to the prior print signals slowing aggregate demand and weaker future earnings, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when GDP weakness is not matched by offsetting labor or production strength.
failure_condition: Reject if sequential GDPC1 contractions are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal disappears whenever INDPRO and PAYEMS are jointly improving.
